title: updelocc
text: updelocc Remove an occurrence of the specified entity from the hitlist. Explain this code long updelocc (unsigned char*Entity, longOccnum); Copy Parameters Entity Name of the entity. You can specify zero or an empty string to denote the current entity Occnum Sequence number of the occurrence to be deleted. Specify -1 to remove the last occurrence in the hitlist, or zero to remove the current occurrence. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by updelocc Value Meaning >0 The sequence number of the occurrence that became current after removing. -1 The occurrence could not be removed. Most likely causes: Occnum is out of range, or Entity does not exist, or it is not painted on the component. Use Library: yrtl Description updelocc removes the occurrence of entity having the sequence number Occnum . If Entity is zero or an empty string, the current entity is used. Note: updelocc returns 1 even when the last occurrence was removed, so you cannot use updelocc in a loop to remove all occurrences. updelocc is equivalent to the ProcScript remocc statement, where you can find more information about the rules for removing occurrences. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void DELOCC () { updelocc(0, 0); /* Revove current occurrence of current entity */ } Copy Related Links upaddocc Create an empty occurrence of an entity. updisocc Discard one or more occurrences of the specified entity from the hitlist. upsetocc Make the specified occurrence current.
